assumptions: The developer has a supported browser, email address, and a device that can install the Twingate Client.
No Twingate account, Remote Network, Resource, Connector, or Client session exists yet.
The developer can deploy a Connector on a host that can reach the private Resource.

developer_goal: Create a Twingate account, protect a private Resource, and access that Resource from the Twingate Client.

first_success.outcome: An authenticated Twingate Client reaches the configured private Resource from the developer's device.
first_success.observable_signal: Once connected and authenticated, the Resource address opens directly from the Client device.

route_selection.rule: Use the official Quick Start after account creation, but only after signup fields and one Connector plus Client platform path are fixed.

candidates.name: Manual Quick Start with free signup
candidates.reason: Official general route for Remote Network, Resource, Connector, and Client access, but signup is an opaque prerequisite and Connector deployment options are environment-specific peers.

candidates.name: Automated Deployment alternative
candidates.reason: Quick Start presents automated deployment as an alternative to the manual general route without ranking it as subordinate for every developer.

candidates.name: API getting started
candidates.reason: Assumes an existing tenant and API key and targets automation rather than first private Resource access.

required_path.action: Sign up for a free Twingate account at the official signup URL.

required_fields.label: Signup and initial tenant fields
required_fields.notes: Quick Start requires an account and links signup, but does not document identity provider choice, verification, or whether a Remote Network is created during signup.

required_path.observable_result: The developer can open the Twingate Admin Console for a new tenant.

required_path.action: Create a Remote Network if signup did not already create one.
required_path.observable_result: A Remote Network exists in the Admin Console.

required_path.action: Add a Resource and authorize the Everyone group.

required_fields.label: Authorized group
required_fields.notes: Everyone is the only available group unless custom groups already exist.

required_path.observable_result: The Resource is created and granted to a group.

required_path.action: Deploy a Connector on a host that can reach the Resource using one of the displayed deployment methods.

required_fields.label: Connector deployment method and host commands
required_fields.notes: Quick Start presents multiple environment-specific Connector options without selecting one universal path.

required_path.observable_result: Connector Connection Status shows successful Controller and Relay connectivity.

required_path.action: Install the Twingate Client and authenticate.

required_fields.label: Client platform install and authentication
required_fields.notes: get.twingate.com covers multiple OS clients; required auth fields are not enumerated in the Quick Start.

required_path.observable_result: The Client is connected and authenticated to the tenant.

required_path.action: Open the configured Resource address from the Client device.
required_path.observable_result: The protected Resource is reachable directly from the device.

prerequisites.description: Sufficient permissions to deploy a Docker container or native Linux service on the Remote Network host.
A private Resource address the Connector host can reach.

external_gates.description: Connector deployment requires administrative access to a reachable remote host outside the Twingate Admin Console.

unavoidable_waits.description: After Connector deploy starts, Connection Status must show Controller and Relay connectivity before the Connector can serve requests.

platform_outcomes.description: A connected Connector provides encrypted access into the Remote Network for authorized Resources.
An authenticated Client can open authorized Resource addresses from the device network path Twingate provides.

excluded.item: Reading Quick Start documentation as a measured action
excluded.reason: Documentation reading is evidence, not a product action.

excluded.item: Team management, security policies, and Services for CI/CD
excluded.reason: Quick Start places these under Next Steps after Resource access.

sources.title: Quick Start
evidence_supported: Account prerequisite and free signup link
Connector deploy options and Connection Status
Client install and Resource access terminal

sources.title: Twingate signup
evidence_supported: Official signup entry URL referenced by Quick Start

uncertainties.question: What is the complete current Twingate signup, identity, verification, and initial Remote Network sequence?
uncertainties.impact: All required account-creation fields and whether a Remote Network is created during signup remain unknown, so the path cannot be verified from account creation.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Dated screenshots or a read-only walkthrough from auth.twingate.com/signup through the Admin Console state that enters Quick Start configuration.

uncertainties.question: Which Connector deployment method and Client platform define the selected first-mile path?
uncertainties.impact: Installation commands, screen counts, and host prerequisites change by environment and OS.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Fix one Connector target (for example Docker on Linux) and one Client OS, then verify the complete deploy and auth steps against current Console UI.
